parsed_review.summary: CP00-731 is a Risk C, descriptor-only RP24 Korean Legal Depth "P02/P03 interface fixture bridge" pack (150 units, RP24.P02.M09.S09-RP24.P03.M06.S12). Read-only inspection of registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, model.test.js, the generated contract, manifest, plan, queue, and README confirms the pack content supports a descriptor-only closeout with no blockers. Directly verified: CP731 deliverable_counts {ui:6, implementation:63, claude_review:6, failure_recovery:4, test:20, contract:31, security_audit:16, hermes_evidence:4} and micro_title_row_counts match the pack baseline exactly and sum to 150 across phases P02(34)+P03(116); the implementation follows the established, already-closed CP722-CP730 pattern with deep-frozen objects and a freezeResult that forces the no-write attestation; validators enforce descriptor_only, no runtime execution, no sensitive payloads, no runtime opening, no cross-tenant/permission/audit-body leakage, Claude non-final, and no enterprise-trust claim; the contract projects latest_pack=CP00-731 with validation.valid=true and CP722-730 retained historical; manifest correctly shows status in_progress / production_ready false / runtime_ready false / writes_product_state false awaiting exactly one valid hardened review (this one); no real-data, secret, or Korean-legal-text leakage exists in packages/korean-legal/src; plan current_pack and queue live_latest_pack_id are both CP00-731 with next unit RP24.P03.M06.S13; and the readiness report shows the auth preflight passing with valid non-zero JSON, clearing the CP178+ invalid-evidence checks. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer and Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane review/evidence pattern (emits_runtime_receipt=false, not embedded in product runtime). Provenance note: the 25 validation-ladder exit codes in command-evidence.json are recorded claims that could not be re-executed under this read-only review, but the directly-inspected structure and boundaries independently corroborate them. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so the human-final-approval authority boundary lies outside this receipt and does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
